title: INDIA bloc meeting concludes, consensus on 5 issues including Pradhan's resignation
content: INDIA bloc leaders met, agreeing to petition the Chief Justice on electoral integrity and demanding the Education Minister's resignation over exam controversies. They also called for an all-party meeting on the economy and pledged to meet bi-monthly. Parliament coordination will continue daily during the monsoon session.

title: Visakhapatnam Steel Plant accident: 8 killed after hot liquid metal spills on workers
content: A tragic accident at Vizag Steel Plant claimed eight lives and injured six workers. A hot metal ladle fell, causing a massive fire in Steel Melting Shop One. Bodies are difficult to identify due to severe burns. Injured workers are receiving treatment for extensive burns. This incident is the plant's worst since a 2012 explosion.

title: 'Does he really want to?': De Villiers has one big 'doubt' about Sooryavanshi
content: Speaking at an event in Mumbai on Sunday, de Villiers said Sooryavanshi has the skill to succeed in Test cricket but questioned whether the teenager would want to take that path.

title: 'Meetings may soon be held in a car': BJP mocks shrinking INDIA bloc
content: The Bharatiya Janata Party has criticized the INDIA bloc's recent meeting in Delhi. BJP MP Sambit Patra stated the opposition alliance has lost public support and credibility. He mocked the shrinking scale of their gatherings. INDIA bloc leaders discussed issues like the NEET-CBSE row and the economy. They plan to meet every two months.

title: Google AI CEO Demis Hassabis on AGI: Humans only have a few years left to prepare
content: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis warns humanity is on the cusp of Artificial General Intelligence, potentially within four years, urging immediate preparation. He views current AI agents as a societal stress test and highlights the risks of recursive self-improvement. Hassabis also criticized tech companies for laying off engineers, advocating for increased productivity instead.

title: Manav Suthar joins Rohit and Shreyas, becomes only the 9th Indian to ...
content: Manav Suthar made a dream Test debut for India. He earned Player of the Match for his outstanding bowling performance. Suthar took seven wickets in the match. India secured a massive innings victory over Afghanistan. His debut effort is among the best by an Indian bowler. Suthar emphasized consistency and patience in Test cricket.

title: SpaceX IPO explained: Can Indians invest and how does it work?
content: SpaceX's monumental $1.75 trillion IPO is generating immense global interest, with bankers reporting double the demand for available shares. While Indian investors cannot directly participate in the IPO allotment, they can invest in SpaceX shares on secondary markets after listing through international brokers under the RBI's LRS scheme.

title: As Jamie Dimon calls Elon Musk 'Edison of our time', Musk says: There will not be much AC left
content: As SpaceX eyes a historic IPO, JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon likened Elon Musk to Thomas Edison. This sparked online debate, with many championing Nikola Tesla. Musk himself weighed in, acknowledging Edison's brilliance while asserting DC's future dominance over AC, citing solar, batteries, and EVs as key drivers.

title: TMC revolt knocks Parliament: 20 MPs seek tie-up with NDA as Mamata attends INDIA meet
content: At least 20 TMC MPs have written to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla expressing their support for the BJP-led NDA, following Rajya Sabha MP Sukhendu Shekhar Ray's resignation. Rebel leader Ritabrata Banerjee cited disillusionment with the party's leadership and parliamentary treatment as reasons for the growing dissent.

title: Chosen one Manav Suthar gives India hope with dip, revs and perfect 'tappa'
content: Manav Suthar on Sunday bagged a six-wicket haul in his debut Test outing. The 23-year-old, who was given the nod ahead of Harsh Dubey for the one-off Test against Afghanistan, became only the tenth Indian bowler and seventh Indian spinner to claim a five-wicket haul on Test debut.

title: The INDIA bloc divide: Why Mamata is all in but Stalin is walking 'ekla chalo' path
content: Both lost power. Both saw their political dominance challenged. Yet while Mamata Banerjee has turned to the INDIA bloc as a source of relevance and protection, MK Stalin has chosen to focus on rebuilding his own turf. Their contrasting responses offer a glimpse into the next phase of opposition politics in India.

title: ‘America’s largest private employer tells its employees AI will not take their jobs’
content: Walmart assures its 2.1 million employees that AI will enhance their roles, not replace them. Company leaders emphasized that while technology will be integral to future operations, human employees will remain central. AI is already being implemented to improve efficiency, such as a tool helping truck drivers optimize loads and reduce empty miles.

title: NFL trade rumors: Cowboys eye $20M raiders defensive upgrade
content: The Dallas Cowboys are once again being linked to Raiders star Maxx Crosby after reportedly trying to trade for him earlier this offseason. ESPN insider Dan Graziano believes a midseason deal remains possible if Las Vegas struggles. Jerry Jones is still viewed as a potential suitor, though any move would require major draft assets and significant salary cap planning for Dallas.

title: ‘Matter of utmost concern’: India urges de-escalation as Israel-Iran exchange fire
content: India has voiced grave concern over escalating Middle East violence, urging immediate de-escalation and diplomatic talks. The ongoing conflict, now over 100 days, is causing immense human suffering and impacting the global economy. Indian missions in Israel and Iran have issued urgent travel and safety advisories for citizens, emphasizing caution and immediate departure where necessary.

title: Bajaj Allianz wins mediclaim dispute after probe finds hospital records manipulated
content: A Delhi consumer court overturned a reimbursement order for a mediclaim, stating an insurer was justified in rejecting a claim. Investigations revealed significant flaws and discrepancies in hospital records, including alleged non-cooperation from the insured. The court emphasized the importance of considering investigator reports, concluding the insurer acted within policy terms.

title: Fitness at what cost? Cardiac emergencies, fatal mishaps put gym safety in spotlight
content: Recent tragic incidents in Indian gyms, including celebrity deaths from cardiac arrest and fatal accidents, highlight critical safety concerns. These events underscore the urgent need for mandatory health screenings, proper supervision, and adherence to safety standards within fitness centers to prevent further avoidable tragedies.

title: Rs 13,000 cr airport at Great Nicobar Island to improve India's forward posture in Indian Ocean
content: India is investing Rs 13,000 crore in Great Nicobar Island for a dual-use airport, bolstering civilian connectivity and military readiness near the vital Strait of Malacca. This strategic project enhances surveillance of crucial shipping lanes, impacting China's energy security and strengthening India's Indo-Pacific influence. The development also includes a trans-shipment hub, aiming to boost India's economic standing.

title: India now has 190 nuclear warheads: What's driving New Delhi's atomic buildup?
content: India's nuclear warheads have increased to 190. This expansion is part of a modernization effort. Global nuclear powers are upgrading their arsenals. Asia faces rising tensions and strategic competition. India's nuclear strategy focuses on China and Pakistan. New delivery systems and sea-based deterrents are being developed. The global arms control framework is weakening.

title: Bengal signature 'forgery' case: Abhishek Banerjee seeks time to appear before CID
content: TMC leader Abhishek Banerjee sought more time from West Bengal CID for questioning in a signature forgery case involving party legislators. The probe concerns alleged forged signatures on documents related to legislative appointments. Banerjee, who was in Delhi for an INDIA bloc meeting, stated he would cooperate after consulting legal advisors, calling the investigation politically motivated.

title: Court orders 10 free biriyani plates for five Sundays after man finds dead insect in food
content: A Puducherry restaurant, Briyani & Co., has been ordered to pay ₹10,000 compensation and provide 10 free biryani plates after a customer found a dead insect in his food. The consumer commission found the restaurant liable for serving contaminated food, deeming it a 'deficiency in service'. The eatery's contradictory responses further influenced the ruling.

title: Gauhati high court grants Rs 25 lakh to widow in alleged custodial death case
content: In a landmark decision, the Gauhati High Court has ordered the Assam government to compensate the widow of a businessman who reportedly faced torture and death while in police custody. This ruling, distinct from ongoing legal actions, underscores the egregious breach of basic human rights.

title: Manav Suthar stars on debut; India crush Afghanistan by an innings and 300 runs
content: India secured a historic innings and 300-run win against Afghanistan in the one-off Test. Debutant Manav Suthar impressed with a seven-wicket match haul. India's batsmen posted a massive total, forcing Afghanistan to follow on. This victory marks India's largest ever in Test cricket, showcasing a dominant performance.

title: Facing a dispute with your builder? Here's how RERA can help register your grievance
content: Homebuyers facing builder issues like delayed possession or unfulfilled amenities can seek recourse through RERA. The article outlines the step-by-step process for filing complaints, including identifying the correct state portal, registering, detailing the grievance, and paying fees. It also highlights state-specific variations in submission methods, guiding consumers towards resolution.

title: Ships attacked in Hormuz: 24 Indian seafarers 'seek urgent assistance'
content: Twenty-four Indian seafarers are urgently seeking help after their vessel was reportedly attacked off Oman's coast, near the Strait of Hormuz. The Forward Seamen's Union of India has appealed to the Indian Navy and authorities for intervention amidst rising regional tensions. Details regarding the attack and the vessel remain unconfirmed by official sources.

title: NEET UG 2026 re-exam under unprecedented security with question paper setters in strict isolation and multi-layer monitoring by NTA
content: Following concerns over a paper leak in NEET-UG 2026, the government and NTA have introduced an unprecedented security framework for the June 21 re-exam. Over 500,000 security personnel, biometric checks, facial recognition, and live CCTV monitoring have been deployed across India and abroad. Question paper setters are kept under strict isolation, and social media is being monitored to prevent misinformation, ensuring a fair, transparent examination for over 2.2 million aspirants nationwide this year in India.

title: ‘Test cricket is about patience’: Manav Suthar caps dream debut with POTM award
content: Manav Suthar made a dream Test debut, claiming seven wickets to guide India to a massive innings and 300-run victory over Afghanistan. The young spinner, named Player of the Match, emphasised patience and consistency as crucial for success in the longest format. His stellar performance, including a first-innings 6 for 33, set the stage for India's dominant win.

title: 'She welcomed them in, offered water': Chilling details of DU professor's final moments
content: A Delhi University professor was brutally murdered by a couple she had welcomed into her home, offering them water. The crime, stemming from a property dispute, saw the accused use forged identities and travel extensively to evade capture. Police apprehended the couple in West Bengal after a multi-state manhunt.

title: 'Stop shooting': Trump demands as Iran, Israel resume strikes 1st time post ceasefire
content: Tensions flared as Iran and Israel exchanged missile and air strikes, shattering an April ceasefire and sparking fears of wider conflict. US President Trump urged an immediate halt to hostilities. The escalating attacks, involving Iranian missile barrages and Israeli strikes on Iranian targets, have disrupted regional stability and rattled global energy markets, with Iraq and Syria closing airspace.

title: India register biggest-ever Test victory with innings win over Afghanistan
content: The win surpassed India's previous biggest Test victory margin and was set up by a dominant bowling performance that saw them take 14 Afghanistan wickets on the third day.

title: Jahangir Khan, TMC's 'Pushpa', arrested at India-Nepal border on 'extortion' charges
content: The arrest comes weeks after Calcutta high court's vacation bench refused to extend the interim protection in five FIRs granted to the TMC strongman. Justice Partha Sarathi Chatterjee referred the matter to a regular bench. Earlier, Khan was granted interim protection by Justice Saugata Bhattacharyya till May 26, considering the repolls there on May 21.

title: 'You don't need a turning track': Gavaskar decodes Suthar's six-wicket haul
content: Debutant Manav Suthar starred as India bowled out Afghanistan for 152 and enforced the follow-on in the lone Test. The left-arm spinner claimed 6 for 33, recording the second-best figures by an Indian on Test debut. Sunil Gavaskar praised Suthar’s accuracy, control and skill, saying his success came from precision and smart bowling rather than pitch assistance.

title: After murder, stayed 30 mins, changed: DU prof’s tenants killed her, brought minor son to 'blend in'
content: A Delhi University professor's murder in east Delhi has been linked to a multi-crore ancestral property in West Bengal. Police arrested a couple from Bardhaman and apprehended their minor son, alleging they killed the professor after she refused to sell her share of the inherited property.

title: Ceasefire gave Iran breathing room: How Tehran used it to replenish its arsenal
content: Following an April 8 ceasefire, Iran has rapidly rebuilt its military might, reopening vital missile and drone storage tunnels. Despite extensive US strikes, Tehran has repaired facilities, reorganized logistics, and accelerated production. With a significant portion of its arsenal intact and drone output high, Iran is leveraging this period to bolster its capabilities and influence regional dynamics.

title: 'It's called 'idli', not 'id-slice': Tharoor dishes out another culinary lesson
content: Congress leader Shashi Tharoor amused netizens with his witty responses to culinary debates on social media. He humorously critiqued an 'idli pizza' cutting style, attributing it to software engineering logic. Previously, Tharoor also defended the South Indian idli against a comparison to rasgulla, highlighting its unique preparation and culinary significance.

title: Watch: CM Vijay plays chess with Praggnanandhaa after announcing Rs 50 lakh reward
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay honoured chess prodigy R. Praggnanandhaa with Rs 50 lakh and a memento after his historic Norway Chess triumph. The 20-year-old became the first Indian to win the title, defeating Magnus Carlsen twice and D. Gukesh. After starting poorly, he staged a remarkable comeback, winning four straight games to secure the championship.

title: Visakhapatnam Steel Plant accident: eight feared dead in ladle blast, fire at SMS-1
content: A ladle of liquid steel explodes at the Continuous Casting Department, sparking a fire and spilling molten steel across the shop floor at the RINL-run plant

title: Two Nellore pilgrims killed in bus accident in Bihar, 15 injured
content: Chief Minister Nara Chandrababu Naidu contacts the Bihar government officials to take necessary emergency measures on a war-footing basis; Endowments Minister expresses deep shock and sorrow over the death of the pilgrims

title: Supreme Court seeks CBSE’s response on plea over withheld result of student based in Saudi Arabia
content: Non-declaration of his results had placed his higher education plans in jeopardy, the Class 12 student has said

title: Trinamool rebellion reaches Delhi: 20 party MPs plan to form a separate bloc and support NDA
content: 20 of the 29 Lok Sabha MPs want to create a separate bloc and support the BJP-led NDA government in Parliament; Barasat Lok Sabha MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar is emerging as the leader of the group; West Bengal CM Suvendu Adhikari and Union Minister Bhupendra Yadav in contact with the rebel camp

title: Rajnath Singh reviews HAL projects; Tejas Mk1A delivery delays come under scrutiny
content: The Minister asks HAL Chairman to strictly adhere to delivery schedules for all defence projects and emphasises need for timely execution of programmes critical to the armed forces’ modernisation plans

title: 1.37 lakh acres excluded from Section 22 (A) list, says A.P. Chief Minister Naidu
content: The government has deployed blockchain technology to ensure data accuracy in pattadar passbooks, says Naidu, adding that all care have been taken to protect land records data from tampering

title: Civil Supplies reforms make A.P. a model State: Manohar
content: Minister cites transparent paddy procurement, timely payments, WhatsApp-based sales and QR code-enabled smart ration cards as key reforms implemented over the past two years

title: Ayurveda guides people towards a healthy life, says Joshi
content: The Union Minister says Ayurveda not only treats diseases but also ensures perfect physical, mental and spiritual balance

title: Firewood returns to school kitchens in Kottayam as LPG availability concerns linger
content: With classes now functioning in full swing, insistence by some school headmasters to use firewood hearth has become a major challenge for cooking workers, says School Pachaka Thozhilali Union

title: Study to highlight Mysuru’s footpath woes; MGP to seek solutions
content: The meeting, titled ‘Giving footpaths back to pedestrians’, will feature preliminary findings from the ongoing study being conducted by students on the latest status of footpaths in the city

title: Second hike amid West Asia crisis pushes domestic LPG price up by ₹89/cylinder
content: Price of a domestic LPG cylinder has gone up by ₹29 after the second hike in three months; Centre says oil marketing companies are now incurring under-recoveries at about ₹700 per cylinder

title: It is a false case aimed at intimidating me, says Dattatraya Patil Revoor
content: The former MLA asserts that the false FIR against him will not deter him from raising issues in public interest, even as he defends his remarks

title: ‘My memories are frozen on June 12’: A year on, families of AI-171 victims still searching for answers
content: While compensation from the airline and government has helped address immediate financial concerns, several relatives of the victims say no amount of money can replace the lives lost in one of India’s worst aviation tragedies

title: Pay ₹10 lakh each to three child victims of sexual offence, orders Madras High Court
content: Justice A.D. Jagadish Chandira directs Tamil Nadu government to pay the amount from the Child Victim Compensation Fund

title: Flex battles, football dreams mark Kerala’s World Cup countdown
content: Hoardings, cut-outs and flags of different teams appear across towns and villages as their fanbases set the ball rolling ahead of the quadrennial event

title: Party high command, CLP will decide CM candidate in 2028: Satish Jarkiholi
content: He was responding to CM Shivakumar’s statement that he will seek the blessings of the people to continue in the post after the 2028 polls

title: Extreme heat tests India's record milk production
content: Extreme heat means cows ‌eat less, make less milk, struggle to conceive and produce fewer live offspring, experts say, while farmers spend more trying to keep animals cool and fertile

title: Foreigners who returned after voting in T.N. Assembly election come under the scanner
content: Central and State law-enforcement agencies are working with the Election Commission of India to obtain data on foreign nationals who might have voted. Investigators have sought the assistance of the Bureau of Immigration in identifying foreign nationals who arrived in the State ahead of the election and departed within two to three days of polling

title: Shivamogga DC convenes meeting to prepare for heavy rain
content: The Indian Meteorological Department has issued a red alert for heavy rainfall expected in the next two days

title: ‘Educated youth should become job givers’
content: 260 students awarded graduation certificates at Jain College of Engineering and Research convocation in Belagavi

title: Centre bets big on coal gasification; targets ₹4 lakh cr investment, ₹3 lakh cr savings, 1 lakh jobs
content: Union Minister G. Kishan Reddy says the Centre is prioritising both surface and underground coal gasification to reduce import dependence while unlocking high-value by-products such as ammonia, methanol, hydrogen, fertilisers, chemicals and petrochemicals

title: Physics tops complaints on CBSE Class XII grading
content: students across social media have alleged missing marks, strict checking, uncredited step-marking and, in some cases, answer-sheet mismatches . many have claimed that complete answers or correctly attempted numerical problems received either partial credit or no marks at all .

title: World comes to China, Xi goes to North Korea: Why Kim Jong Un makes Beijing uneasy
content: Aashish Saxena is an Assistant News Editor at the Times of India. He has 14 years of editing and reporting experience in both print and digital media. He writes extensively on geopolitics, Indian politics, defence tech, and current affairs in South Asia and Middle East. Read More

title: SWAMIH Fund explained: How it works, eligibility, and what's next
content: The TOI Business Desk is a vigilant and dedicated team of journalists committed to delivering the latest and most relevant business news from around the world to readers of The Times of India. The primary focus of the TOI Business Desk is to keep a watchful eye on the global business landscape, covering a wide spectrum of industries, markets, economic trends, in-depth analysis, exclusive reports and breaking stories that impact businesses and economies. With a mission to provide valuable insights and updates, the desk ensures that TOI readers are well-informed about the ever-changing and dynamic world of commerce and can navigate the complexities of the business world. title: "Help, Help": New Video Shows Desperate Moments Inside Deadly Delhi Hotel Fire
content: New visuals have emerged from the site of the devastating fire that tore through a hotel in South Delhi's congested Hauz Rani area of Malviya Nagar on Wednesday, killing 21 people, including nine Indians and 12 foreign nationals.

title: The Inside Story Of Rivalry Between Khan Sir And Raushan Sir
content: While the recent dispute may have been triggered by the removal or installation of posters, there is a long backstory to it

title: What Forced Nearly 100 Schools To Shut In Japan? A Bear
content: A bear roaming the streets of a Japanese city for three days forced authorities to shut nearly 100 schools on Monday as hunters, police and local officials launched a major search operation.

title: Trinamool's Kakoli Dastidar Says 20 MPs Will Support NDA: "Written To Speaker"
content: Trinamool MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar, who was upset with the party after being removed from the post of the Whip, said going by the people's verdict, they believe that their "future political course should be aligned with the NDA".

title: 15-Minute Rahul Gandhi Speech, Unity Call: Inside Details Of INDIA Bloc Meet
content: During his nearly 15-minute address, Rahul Gandhi is learnt to have expressed disappointment over the lack of support from some partners on issues such as alleged "vote chori" and also took aim at parties that frequently criticise the Congress.

title: Hardeep Puri Explains How India Is Rewriting Its Energy Map As Wars Rage On
content: Before November 2022, Russia accounted for just 0.2 per cent of India's crude imports. Today, Puri confirmed, India is buying approximately 2.5 million barrels per day from Russia, a 12-fold increase.

title: Opinion: Opinion | How A Photograph in Delhi Says Something Important About Trump's America
content: During her visit to New Delhi this week, Venezuela's Delcy Rodrguez, met US Ambassador Sergio Gor. Had the meeting taken place in Washington or Caracas, it would have attracted little attention. In Delhi, however, it carried a different significance.

title: Willing To Work Together: China's Xi Jinping To North Korea's Kim Jong Un
content: The two sides should strengthen exchanges in diplomacy, law enforcement, and the military, Chinese President Xi Jinping said.

title: Several MPs Suffer High-Altitude Sickness During Official Ladakh Tour
content: Owing to low oxygen levels at high altitude, the MPs experienced severe breathing difficulties and increased heart rate.

title: US Iran War Live: Iranian Military Announces 'Cessation' Of Offensives On Israel
content: US Iran War LIVE Updates: Iran closed the airspace around Tehran's Imam Khomeini International Airport, the country's main airfield, after the Israeli attack.

title: Chagos Islands: Military Power, Colonial Legacy, And A Billion-Dollar Deal
content: The US officials are reportedly looking at ways to deal directly with Mauritius instead of going through the United Kingdom.

title: Cooker Gave It Away: How A Whistle Led Police To Kerala Man's Cannabis Stash
content: The 40-year-old accused was arrested on the spot. A total of 400 grams of cannabis was recovered from his possession.

title: "Delhi Will Choke": High Court On Centre's Eviction Notice To Polo Body
content: The Delhi High Court said green spaces in the national capital were already rare, and now whatever "little green space" remains in the New Delhi Municipal Corporation (NDMC) areas is also being taken

title: Manager Surrenders 5 Days After 22 Die In Delhi Hotel Fire
content: Though Lavkesh Bajaj is the sole owner of the B&B, he had handed over the entire management to manager Jai Mishra.

title: 'No Right To Breathe Freely': Afghan Women Detained In Clothing Crackdown
content: Taliban authorities rule according to a strict interpretation of Islamic law, and have gradually tightened restrictions on women since returning to power in August 2021.

title: Trump Warns Israel, Iran To "Stop Shooting", Then Makes A Ceasefire Claim
content: Israel and Iran fired missiles at each other on Monday in the most serious escalation of conflict after a ceasefire was announced in April.

title: 3 Air India A320 Aircraft Damaged At Delhi Airport After Strong Winds
content: Three Air India A320 aircraft parked at Delhi airport's Terminal 2 were damaged after strong winds during adverse weather conditions sent ground equipment hurtling into parked planes on Sunday afternoon.
